epic.arc: The foreign review of exports/program-as-prompt/ comes home as work. Each finding is a proposed atom under this one arc; bdo's confirm-arc queues them all, his decline retires them — either way the review stops stranding as context.
epic.context: Returned through the inbound envoy seam (done-line 0059) against seal 793fc274baca… on exports/log.jsonl. The reviewer saw a sealed snapshot, so every finding is PROPOSED and unverified: the value gate judges each story and a session verifies its claim against the live repo before any is acted on.
epic.value: A foreign review becomes value-gated, queued work instead of read-only context — the return leg the one-way envoy lacked.
epic.horizon: Every finding judged by the value gate and either carried under bdo's confirmed arc or refused; none hand-carried into a doc.

pieces.glue: Foreign proposal: formalize the 1.0/3.0 seam as a named-holes spec — a deterministic spine (rules, control-flow, caps/budgets, schemas, traceable outputs) exposing typed INFER ports (priority/risk/meaning) that alone accept inference, plus an Agent-Rights model OBEY/INFER/AUDIT/REFUSE.

Foreign proposal: treat the topology diagram as the executable artifact — nodes are loops, edges are routing, holes are named inference ports, trace is proof the topology ran, receipt is reusable residue.

Foreign proposal: reframe program-as-prompt as a Bootable Agent Environment — an 8-step boot pack (world-snapshot, topology and authority, typed-loops, claim, execute-and-trace, self-audit, reject/repair/accept, residual-value) that loops; install a working reality, not just a prompt.

Foreign proposal: a coordinator plus scout/builder/reviewer/repairer fleet acting on shared repo/queue/tests/logs/acceptance-gate under fleet rules (bounded authority, shared state, visible receipts, no second truth).

Foreign proposal: a runnable .boot package (runner, topology, fixture, equal-info prose control, blind-judge) implementing the A/B — Arm A runs the topology, Arm B is equal prose, strip labels, blind-judge whether Arm A actually ran the topology.
